Construct an anonymous function in Python's idiomatic programming environment, aiming to determine whether a given variable represents a prime number or rather a composite number, employing the modulus operator as a means for this differentiation. * * *  
The function should check if a number is divisible by any integer other than 1 and itself, using the modulus operator to determine divisibility. The status of the number is determined by whether it has any divisors other than 1 and the number itself.  
The function is written using a lambda expression, and it returns "prime" if the number is not divisible by any number up to its square root. The status of the number is determined by whether it has any divisors other than 1 and the number itself.
